topic: Domain-Specific AI Agent Architecture for Automated Sloshing Simulation: Enabling Non-Expert CFD Analysis through Tool-Augmented Local SLM
motivation: Sloshing simulation using DualSPHysics requires expert knowledge in SPH parameters, XML case configuration, mesh sensitivity, and post-processing — creating a significant barrier for non-expert engineers. Existing LLM-for-CFD approaches (OpenFOAMGPT, Foam-Agent, ChatCFD) target OpenFOAM with cloud-based proprietary models, leaving DualSPHysics (GPU-accelerated SPH) completely unaddressed. No prior work combines local SLM deployment with domain-specific tool augmentation for particle-based CFD solvers.
hypothesis: A domain-specific AI agent (SlosimAgent) powered by a locally-deployed Qwen3-32B SLM, augmented with 18 specialized tools via MCP protocol and guided by a ReAct reasoning loop with sloshing-specific domain prompts, can enable non-expert users to configure, execute, and analyze DualSPHysics sloshing simulations with comparable fidelity to expert-configured cases, while maintaining full data privacy through local inference.
existing_approaches: OpenFOAMGPT 2.0 (2025): Multi-agent framework for OpenFOAM, GPT-4o, 100% success on 450+ sims
Foam-Agent 2.0 (2025): Claude 3.5 Sonnet, 88.2% success, HPC deployment
ChatCFD (2025-2026): DeepSeek-R1/V3, 82.1% execution, 68.12% physics fidelity, 315 benchmarks
NL2FOAM (2025): Qwen2.5-7B fine-tuned on 28,716 CFD cases, 88.7% accuracy
SimLLM (2025): Multi-stage SFT+DPO for SimPy, Qwen2.5-Coder-7B
MetaOpenFOAM (2024): MetaGPT assembly line paradigm for OpenFOAM
ChemCrow (2023): 13 domain-specific chemistry tools with LLM agent
